title: paspalum longe-pedunculatum
intro: Paspalum longe-pedunculatum, also known as Bahama grass, is a type of tropical grass native to the Americas. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Paspalum longe-pedunculatum are said to include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in wound healing. It is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, the leaves are used to make a poultice for topical application.
